title: UTKa DataLab
text: UTKa DataLab is a joint research initiative between Kadaster and the University of Twente, based in Apeldoorn. We develop methods to extract, combine, and analyze information from distributed data sources, with a strong commitment to ethical conduct, scientific integrity, and open science.

title: UTKa DataLab
text:
Research Themes

AI with Authoritative Data — grounding LLMs and AI systems in authoritative knowledge graphs
Trusted Federative Data Infrastructures — secure, privacy-respecting architectures for cross-system data sharing

title: A Federated Knowledge Graph Architecture for Dutch Cadastral Data
text:
← Back to Works

Kadaster · University of Twente


Proposes a federated virtual knowledge graph architecture that enables interoperable querying over Dutch cadastral data sources without centralizing the underlying datasets. The architecture leverages ontology-based data access (OBDA) principles and SPARQL federation to connect heterogeneous land registration systems in a privacy-respecting manner.







Use your browser’s built-in controls to zoom, scroll, or download the PDF.




<p>
Your browser does not support embedded PDFs. <a href="../papers/A%20Federated%20Knowledge%20Graph%20Architecture%20for%20Dutch%20Cadastral%20Data.pdf">Download the PDF</a> to view it.
</p>

title: Who Lives Where?
text:
← Back to Works

EDOC Forum 2026 · Kadaster · University of Twente


Investigates how authoritative residential registration data can be queried and reasoned over in a knowledge graph setting, with a focus on data quality and federated access. The paper explores challenges in representing uncertain or historical address linkages and presents an approach grounded in the Dutch basic registration infrastructure (BRP/BAG).







Use your browser’s built-in controls to zoom, scroll, or download the PDF.

Your browser does not support embedded PDFs. <a href="../papers/Who_Lives_Where___EDOC_Forum_2026.pdf">Download the PDF</a> to view it.

title: Team
text: Our lab consists of 2 PhD candidates and 3 Engineering Doctorate (EngD) students, all supervised jointly by researchers from Kadaster and the University of Twente.

title: Team
text:
Researchers





Lexi (Alexandra) Rowland ✉ 
PhD Candidate · University of Twente
Security and privacy-aware virtual knowledge graphs — ontology-based policy enforcement and LLM integration.
Project: Security & Privacy-Aware VKGs







Anagha Phaniraj ✉ 
PhD Candidate · University of Twente
Improving LLM pre-training and interpretability using knowledge graphs for land administration domain reasoning.
Project: LLM Enhancement via Knowledge Graphs







Sven Mol ✉ 
Engineering Doctorate · University of Twente
VKG infrastructure design, federated querying, and performance evaluation for legacy systems at Kadaster.
Project: VKGs for Legacy Systems







Charlotte (Lei) Wang ✉ 
Engineering Doctorate · University of Twente
KG-enhanced LLM chatbot for natural language access to land administration information.
Project: LLM & KG Chatbot







Stefan Bussemaker ✉ 
Engineering Doctorate · University of Twente
Transparent AI system design for automated information extraction from notarial deeds.
Project: AI Extraction from Notarial Deeds

title: Research
text: UTKa DataLab’s research is organized around two themes, together spanning five active projects across PhD and EngD tracks.

title: Research
text:
AI with Authoritative Data
We investigate how Large Language Models and AI systems can be made more reliable and trustworthy when grounded in authoritative knowledge graphs. This addresses core challenges in government data applications, including hallucinations and limited explainability in AI-generated outputs. A key example of such a government knowledge graph is the Kadaster Knowledge Graph (Ronzhin et al. 2019).

title: Research
text:
Trusted Federative Data Infrastructures
We design secure data-sharing architectures that respect privacy and data sovereignty. Our work centers on Virtual Knowledge Graph (VKG) infrastructures that enable efficient cross-system querying without requiring data replication or centralization.
